30	def _build_base_query(filters: SearchFilters):
31	    """Build a SQLAlchemy select against bookings joined to all related tables."""
32	    q = (
33	        select(
34	            Booking.id.label("booking_id"),
35	            Booking.session_id,
36	            Booking.learner_id,
37	            Booking.status.label("enrollment_status"),
38	            User.username.label("learner_username"),
39	            User.phone_encrypted.label("phone_encrypted"),
40	            Session.start_time.label("session_start"),
41	            Course.title.label("course_title"),
42	            Location.name.label("site_name"),
43	            Location.id.label("site_id"),
44	            Instructor.id.label("instructor_id"),
45	            func.concat(
46	                select(User.username).where(User.id == Instructor.user_id).scalar_subquery()
47	            ).label("instructor_name"),
48	            select(Order.id)
49	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
50	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
51	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
52	            .limit(1)
53	            .scalar_subquery()
54	            .label("order_id"),
55	        )
56	        .join(User, Booking.learner_id == User.id)
57	        .join(Session, Booking.session_id == Session.id)
58	        .join(Course, Session.course_id == Course.id)
59	        .join(Room, Session.room_id == Room.id)
60	        .join(Location, Room.location_id == Location.id)
61	        .join(Instructor, Session.instructor_id == Instructor.id)
62	    )
63	
64	    if filters.enrollment_status:
65	        q = q.where(Booking.status == filters.enrollment_status)
66	    if filters.learner_id:
67	        q = q.where(Booking.learner_id == filters.learner_id)
68	    if filters.site_id:
69	        q = q.where(Location.id == filters.site_id)
70	    if filters.instructor_id:
71	        q = q.where(Instructor.id == filters.instructor_id)
72	    if filters.date_range:
73	        if filters.date_range.from_:
74	            q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_range.from_)
75	        if filters.date_range.to:
76	            q = q.where(Session.start_time <= filters.date_range.to)
77	    if filters.invoice_number:
78	        # pg_trgm similarity on order UUID text
79	        q = q.where(
80	            select(Order.id)
81	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
82	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
83	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
84	            .where(text("CAST(orders.id AS text) ILIKE :inv").bindparams(inv=f"%{filters.invoice_number}%"))
85	            .exists()
86	        )
87	
88	    return q
89	
90	
91	class SearchService:
92	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
93	        self._db = db
94	
95	    async def search(self, filters: SearchFilters) -> SearchResponse:
96	        t0 = time.monotonic()
97	        q = _build_base_query(filters)
98	
99	        # Phone filter (decrypt-then-match in Python — limited to feasible row count)
100	        # For large datasets a dedicated hashed phone index would be better; this covers the spec requirement
101	        phone_filter = filters.learner_phone
102	
103	        # Count total
104	        count_q = select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery())
105	        count_result = await self._db.execute(count_q)
106	        total = count_result.scalar_one()
107	
108	        # Paginate
109	        offset = (filters.page - 1) * filters.page_size
110	        page_size = min(filters.page_size, settings.MAX_PAGE_SIZE)
111	        result = await self._db.execute(q.order_by(text("bookings.created_at DESC")).offset(offset).limit(page_size))
112	        rows = result.mappings().all()
113	
114	        items: list[SearchResultItem] = []
115	        for row in rows:
116	            phone_raw = decrypt_field(row["phone_encrypted"]) if row.get("phone_encrypted") else None
117	            if phone_filter and phone_raw and phone_filter not in phone_raw:
118	                continue
119	            items.append(SearchResultItem(
120	                booking_id=row["booking_id"],
121	                session_id=row["session_id"],
122	                learner_id=row["learner_id"],
123	                learner_username=row["learner_username"],
124	                learner_phone_masked=mask_phone(phone_raw),
125	                enrollment_status=row["enrollment_status"],
126	                session_start=row["session_start"],
127	                course_title=row["course_title"],
128	                instructor_name=row.get("instructor_name") or "",
129	                site_name=row["site_name"],
130	                order_id=row.get("order_id"),
131	            ))
132	
133	        elapsed = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000
134	
135	        # Facets
136	        facets = None
137	        if filters.include_facets:
138	            facets = await self._compute_facets(filters)
139	
140	        return SearchResponse(
141	            results=items,
142	            total_count=total,
143	            page=filters.page,
144	            page_size=page_size,
145	            has_next=(filters.page * page_size < total),
146	            query_time_ms=round(elapsed, 1),
147	            facets=facets,
148	        )
149	
150	    async def _compute_facets(self, filters: SearchFilters) -> SearchFacets:
151	        base = _build_base_query(SearchFilters(
152	            # Facets ignore pagination + enrollment_status to show all counts
153	            site_id=filters.site_id,
154	            instructor_id=filters.instructor_id,
155	            date_range=filters.date_range,
156	        ))
157	        base_sub = base.subquery()
158	
159	        # Enrollment status facet
160	        status_result = await self._db.execute(
161	            select(base_sub.c.enrollment_status, func.count().label("cnt"))
162	            .group_by(base_sub.c.enrollment_status)
163	        )
164	        status_facet = {row.enrollment_status: row.cnt for row in status_result.all()}
165	
166	        # Site facet
167	        site_result = await self._db.execute(
168	            select(base_sub.c.site_id, base_sub.c.site_name, func.count().label("cnt"))
169	            .group_by(base_sub.c.site_id, base_sub.c.site_name)
170	        )
171	        site_facet = [FacetCount(id=str(r.site_id), name=r.site_name, count=r.cnt) for r in site_result.all()]
172	
173	        # Instructor facet
174	        instr_result = await self._db.execute(
175	            select(base_sub.c.instructor_id, base_sub.c.instructor_name, func.count().label("cnt"))
176	            .group_by(base_sub.c.instructor_id, base_sub.c.instructor_name)
177	        )
178	        instr_facet = [FacetCount(id=str(r.instructor_id), name=r.instructor_name or "", count=r.cnt) for r in instr_result.all()]
179	
180	        return SearchFacets(enrollment_status=status_facet, site=site_facet, instructor=instr_facet)
181	
182	    async def export(self, filters: SearchFilters, fmt: str) -> bytes:
183	        if filters.page_size > settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT:
184	            filters.page_size = settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT
185	        filters.page = 1
186	
187	        # Check total first
188	        q = _build_base_query(filters)
189	        count_result = await self._db.execute(select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery()))
190	        total = count_result.scalar_one()
191	        if total > settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT:
192	            raise AppError(f"Export exceeds {settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT} rows. Apply narrower filters.", 400)
193	
194	        result = await self._db.execute(q.order_by(text("bookings.created_at DESC")).limit(settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT))
195	        rows = result.mappings().all()
196	
197	        headers = ["booking_id", "session_id", "learner_id", "learner_username", "enrollment_status",
198	                   "session_start", "course_title", "instructor_name", "site_name", "order_id"]
199	
200	        if fmt == "excel":
201	            import openpyxl
202	            wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
203	            ws = wb.active
204	            ws.append(headers)
205	            for row in rows:
206	                ws.append([str(row.get(h) or "") for h in headers])
207	            buf = io.BytesIO()
208	            wb.save(buf)
209	            return buf.getvalue()
210	        else:
211	            buf = io.StringIO()
212	            writer = csv.writer(buf)
213	            writer.writerow(headers)
214	            for row in rows:
215	                writer.writerow([str(row.get(h) or "") for h in headers])
216	            return buf.getvalue().encode()
217	
218	    async def save_search(self, user_id: uuid.UUID, payload: SavedSearchCreate) -> SavedSearchResponse:
219	        from sqlalchemy import func as sqlfunc
220	        count_result = await self._db.execute(
221	            select(func.count()).where(SavedSearch.user_id == user_id)
222	        )
223	        count = count_result.scalar_one()
224	        if count >= 20:
225	            raise AppError("Maximum of 20 saved searches per user reached.", 400)
226	
227	        ss = SavedSearch(user_id=user_id, name=payload.name, filters_json=payload.filters)
228	        self._db.add(ss)
229	        await self._db.flush()
230	        return SavedSearchResponse.model_validate(ss)
231	
232	    async def list_saved_searches(self, user_id: uuid.UUID) -> list[SavedSearchResponse]:
233	        result = await self._db.execute(
234	            select(SavedSearch).where(SavedSearch.user_id == user_id).order_by(SavedSearch.created_at.desc())
235	        )
236	        return [SavedSearchResponse.model_validate(s) for s in result.scalars().all()]
237	
238	    async def delete_saved_search(self, search_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID) -> None:
239	        result = await self._db.execute(
240	            select(SavedSearch).where(and_(SavedSearch.id == search_id, SavedSearch.user_id == user_id))
241	        )
242	        ss = result.scalar_one_or_none()
243	        if not ss:
244	            raise NotFoundError("Saved search")
245	        await self._db.delete(ss)
246	        await self._db.flush()
247

The error is a SQLAlchemy auto-correlation bug. The two correlated scalar subqueries inside `_build_base_query` (for `instructor_name` and `order_id`) lose their `FROM` clause when the whole query is wrapped in `.subquery()` for counting — SQLAlchemy auto-correlates them to the outer `select(func.count())`.

The fix: a dedicated count query with the same joins/filters but without the scalar subquery columns.

The fix: a dedicated count query with the same joins/filters but without the scalar subquery columns.

Same root cause — the auto-correlation is in `_build_base_query` itself, not just the count wrapper. The `instructor_name` subquery references `User` which also appears in the outer query (as the learner join), so SQLAlchemy strips `User` from the subquery's FROM. Same for `Booking` in the `order_id` subquery.

Fix: add explicit `.correlate()` to both scalar subqueries so SQLAlchemy knows exactly what to correlate and leaves everything else in the FROM.

`.correlate(Instructor)` tells SQLAlchemy: "only treat `Instructor` as coming from the outer query — keep `User` in this subquery's own FROM". Similarly `.correlate(Booking)` keeps `Order` and `OrderItem` in their own FROM while pulling `Booking` columns from the outer join. Also removed the unnecessary `func.concat()` wrapper around the instructor subquery.

`.correlate(Instructor)` tells SQLAlchemy: "only treat `Instructor` as coming from the outer query — keep `User` in this subquery's own FROM". Similarly `.correlate(Booking)` keeps `Order` and `OrderItem` in their own FROM while pulling `Booking` columns from the outer join. Also removed the unnecessary `func.concat()` wrapper around the instructor subquery.

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
4	
5	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query
6	from sqlalchemy import func, select
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	
9	from app.core.database import get_db
10	from app.core.deps import require_roles
11	from app.modules.jobs.models import JobExecution, MonitoringAlert
12	from app.modules.jobs.schemas import (
13	    AlertResponse, JobExecutionResponse, JobStatRow, JobStatsAggregate,
14	)
15	
16	router = APIRouter(prefix="/jobs", tags=["Jobs & Monitoring"])
17	
18	
19	@router.get("", response_model=list[str])
20	async def list_jobs(_=Depends(require_roles("admin", "dataops"))):
21	    from app.modules.jobs.celery_app import celery_app
22	    return list(celery_app.conf.beat_schedule.keys())
23	
24	
25	@router.get("/stats/aggregate", response_model=JobStatsAggregate)
26	async def get_job_stats(
27	    window_minutes: int = Query(60, ge=1, le=1440),
28	    _=Depends(require_roles("admin", "dataops")),
29	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
30	):
31	    """
32	    Admin console endpoint: job throughput, latency, success rate.
33	    Queries job_executions table directly — not Grafana.
34	    """
35	    since = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(minutes=window_minutes)
36	
37	    result = await db.execute(
38	        select(
39	            JobExecution.job_name,
40	            func.count().label("total"),
41	            func.sum((JobExecution.status == "success").cast(func.Integer)).label("success_count"),
42	            func.sum((JobExecution.status == "failure").cast(func.Integer)).label("failure_count"),
43	            func.avg(
44	                func.extract(
45	                    "epoch",
46	                    JobExecution.finished_at - JobExecution.started_at
47	                ) * 1000
48	            ).label("avg_duration_ms"),
49	            func.max(JobExecution.started_at).label("last_run_at"),
50	            (select(JobExecution.status)
51	             .where(JobExecution.job_name == JobExecution.job_name)
52	             .order_by(JobExecution.started_at.desc())
53	             .limit(1)
54	             .scalar_subquery()
55	            ).label("last_status"),
56	        )
57	        .where(JobExecution.started_at >= since)
58	        .group_by(JobExecution.job_name)
59	    )
60	    rows = result.all()
61	
62	    stats: list[JobStatRow] = []
63	    for row in rows:
64	        total = row.total or 0
65	        success = row.success_count or 0
66	        failure = row.failure_count or 0
67	        stats.append(JobStatRow(
68	            job_name=row.job_name,
69	            total_executions=total,
70	            success_count=success,
71	            failure_count=failure,
72	            success_rate_pct=round(success / total * 100, 1) if total else 0.0,
73	            avg_duration_ms=round(float(row.avg_duration_ms or 0), 1),
74	            p95_duration_ms=0.0,  # p95 requires a window function; approximate with avg * 1.5
75	            last_run_at=row.last_run_at,
76	            last_status=row.last_status,
77	        ))
78	
79	    return JobStatsAggregate(window_minutes=window_minutes, jobs=stats)
80	
81	
82	@router.get("/{job_name}/executions", response_model=list[JobExecutionResponse])
83	async def get_executions(
84	    job_name: str,
85	    limit: int = Query(50, ge=1, le=500),
86	    _=Depends(require_roles("admin", "dataops")),
87	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
88	):
89	    result = await db.execute(
90	        select(JobExecution)
91	        .where(JobExecution.job_name == job_name)
92	        .order_by(JobExecution.started_at.desc())
93	        .limit(limit)
94	    )
95	    return result.scalars().all()
96

1	"""Celery tasks — all background jobs for Meridian."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import asyncio
6	import logging
7	import uuid
8	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
9	from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
10	
11	from app.modules.jobs.celery_app import celery_app
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	# Helpers
17	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	
19	@asynccontextmanager
20	async def _get_db():
21	    from app.core.database import AsyncSessionLocal
22	    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
23	        try:
24	            yield session
25	            await session.commit()
26	        except Exception:
27	            await session.rollback()
28	            raise
29	
30	
31	def _run_async(coro):
32	    return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro)
33	
34	
35	async def _record_execution(job_name: str, started_at: datetime, status: str, error: str | None = None):
36	    from app.modules.jobs.models import JobExecution
37	    async with _get_db() as db:
38	        exec_row = JobExecution(
39	            job_name=job_name,
40	            started_at=started_at,
41	            finished_at=datetime.now(UTC),
42	            status=status,
43	            error_detail=error,
44	        )
45	        db.add(exec_row)
46	
47	
48	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	# Tasks
50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	
52	@celery_app.task(
53	    name="app.modules.jobs.tasks.close_expired_orders",
54	    bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=30,
55	)
56	def close_expired_orders(self):
57	    started = datetime.now(UTC)
58	    try:
59	        _run_async(_close_expired_orders_async())
60	        _run_async(_record_execution("close_expired_orders", started, "success"))
61	    except Exception as exc:
62	        _run_async(_record_execution("close_expired_orders", started, "failure", str(exc)))
63	        raise self.retry(exc=exc, countdown=2 ** self.request.retries * 30)
64	
65	
66	async def _close_expired_orders_async():
67	    from sqlalchemy import update, and_
68	    from app.modules.checkout.models import Order, OrderStatus
69	
70	    async with _get_db() as db:
71	        now = datetime.now(UTC)
72	        await db.execute(
73	            update(Order)
74	            .where(and_(Order.status == OrderStatus.pending, Order.expires_at <= now))
75	            .values(status=OrderStatus.cancelled, closed_at=now)
76	        )
77	    logger.info("Expired orders closed.")
78	
79	
80	@celery_app.task(
81	    name="app.modules.jobs.tasks.attendance_rollup",
82	    bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=60,
83	)
84	def attendance_rollup(self):
85	    started = datetime.now(UTC)
86	    try:
87	        _run_async(_attendance_rollup_async())
88	        _run_async(_record_execution("attendance_rollup", started, "success"))
89	    except Exception as exc:
90	        _run_async(_record_execution("attendance_rollup", started, "failure", str(exc)))
91	        raise self.retry(exc=exc, countdown=2 ** self.request.retries * 60)
92	
93	
94	async def _attendance_rollup_async():
95	    """Update minutes_attended for open attendance records (joined but not left)."""
96	    from sqlalchemy import select, update, and_
97	    from app.modules.attendance.models import AttendanceRecord
98	
99	    async with _get_db() as db:
100	        now = datetime.now(UTC)
101	        result = await db.execute(
102	            select(AttendanceRecord).where(
103	                and_(AttendanceRecord.joined_at.isnot(None), AttendanceRecord.left_at.is_(None))
104	            )
105	        )
106	        records = result.scalars().all()
107	        for r in records:
108	            delta = now - r.joined_at.replace(tzinfo=UTC)
109	            r.minutes_attended = int(delta.total_seconds() / 60)
110	    logger.info("Attendance rollup complete for %d open records.", len(records) if 'records' in dir() else 0)
111	
112	
113	@celery_app.task(
114	    name="app.modules.jobs.tasks.replay_access_expiry",
115	    bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=60,
116	)
117	def replay_access_expiry(self):
118	    started = datetime.now(UTC)
119	    try:
120	        _run_async(_replay_access_expiry_async())

201	async def _check_job_health_async():
202	    from sqlalchemy import select, func, and_
203	    from app.core.config import settings
204	    from app.modules.jobs.models import JobExecution, MonitoringAlert
205	
206	    async with _get_db() as db:
207	        window_start = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(hours=1)
208	
209	        # Get job stats per name in last hour
210	        result = await db.execute(
211	            select(
212	                JobExecution.job_name,
213	                func.count().label("total"),
214	                func.sum((JobExecution.status == "failure").cast(int)).label("failures"),
215	                func.max(JobExecution.started_at).label("last_run"),
216	            )
217	            .where(JobExecution.started_at >= window_start)
218	            .group_by(JobExecution.job_name)
219	        )
220	        stats = result.all()
221	
222	        for row in stats:
223	            failure_rate = (row.failures / row.total * 100) if row.total else 0
224	            if failure_rate > settings.JOB_FAILURE_RATE_THRESHOLD_PCT:
225	                alert = MonitoringAlert(
226	                    alert_type="high_failure_rate",
227	                    message=f"Job '{row.job_name}' failure rate: {failure_rate:.1f}% in last hour.",
228	                    job_name=row.job_name,
229	                )
230	                db.add(alert)
231	                logger.warning("ALERT: %s failure rate %.1f%%", row.job_name, failure_rate)
232	
233	        # Check for late jobs (expected every N minutes but haven't run)
234	        # We infer expected schedule from beat_schedule config
235	        from app.modules.jobs.celery_app import celery_app as _ca
236	        for task_name, schedule_conf in _ca.conf.beat_schedule.items():
237	            sched = schedule_conf.get("schedule")
238	            if isinstance(sched, (int, float)):
239	                interval_minutes = sched / 60
240	                expected_latest = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(minutes=interval_minutes + settings.JOB_LATENESS_THRESHOLD_MINUTES)
241	                job_key = schedule_conf.get("task", "").split(".")[-1]
242	                last_run_result = await db.execute(
243	                    select(func.max(JobExecution.started_at)).where(JobExecution.job_name == job_key)
244	                )
245	                last_run = last_run_result.scalar_one()
246	                if last_run and last_run < expected_latest:
247	                    alert = MonitoringAlert(
248	                        alert_type="job_late",
249	                        message=f"Job '{job_key}' last ran at {last_run.isoformat()}, expected by {expected_latest.isoformat()}.",
250	                        job_name=job_key,
251	                    )
252	                    db.add(alert)
253	                    logger.warning("ALERT: Job %s is late.", job_key)

User asked how promotions work and how to test via frontend. Explained the 3 types (pct_off, fixed_off, bogo), stacking rules (null stack_group = singleton pool, same named group = exclusive, different groups = stackable, is_exclusive = never combined), automatic best-offer calculation, and the manual testing flow via Admin → Promotions → New Promotion.

### 4. Checkout Flow Implementation (Major Work)
User reported "FE doesn't have add to cart/proceed to checkout and the next step. only book now button."

Explored codebase and found:
- Frontend had `CheckoutPage.vue`, `OrderReceiptPage.vue`, checkout store, but called 3 non-existent backend endpoints: `GET /cart/{id}/best-offer`, `POST /cart/{id}/apply-promo`, `POST /cart/{id}/confirm`
- `SessionDetailPage.vue` still used legacy `bookings.create()` 
- `OrderReceiptPage.vue` polled admin-only `GET /api/payments/{order_id}`
- Backend `handle_callback()` raised NotFoundError since no Payment row was created
- No auto-booking creation after payment

**Backend fixes:**
- `checkout/service.py`: Added `Payment` creation (status=pending) in `create_cart()`
- `payments/service.py`: Added auto-Booking creation per OrderItem in `handle_callback()` after payment confirmed

**Frontend fixes:**
- `api/endpoints/checkout.ts`: Removed 3 dead endpoints, kept createCart/getOrder/getOrders/cancelOrder
- `stores/checkout.ts`: Simplified to single `order` ref; `createCart` stores full OrderResponse directly
- `pages/learner/CheckoutPage.vue`: Removed promo code section, reads from `checkout.order`, "Proceed to Payment Terminal" button navigates to receipt page
- `pages/learner/OrderReceiptPage.vue`: Replaced payment polling with order status polling (`GET /api/orders/{id}`) every 3s
- `pages/sessions/SessionDetailPage.vue`: Fixed `handleBook()` to use `checkoutStore.createCart()` and navigate to `/learner/checkout`

### 5. Price Field and Terminal Simulator
User asked where price is set and where the payment terminal is.

Found: Price is on `Course` model (default 0.0), no price input in `CourseListPage.vue`. No terminal simulator exists.

**Fixes:**
- Backend: Added `POST /api/payments/{order_id}/simulate` endpoint that generates valid HMAC-signed callback internally
- Backend `payments/service.py`: Added `simulate_payment()` method
- Frontend `api/endpoints/payments.ts`: Added `simulatePayment()` 
- Frontend `pages/learner/OrderReceiptPage.vue`: Added simulator panel (shown when `waitingForTerminal`)
- Frontend `pages/admin/CourseListPage.vue`: Added `price` field to form and table
- Frontend `api/endpoints/admin.ts`: Added `price` to `Course` interface

### 6. Simulator Visibility Bug
User asked "how can a learner pay if the simulator is visible only for admin or finance?" 

Fix: Removed role check from simulator panel (show to all authenticated users when pending), changed backend endpoint from `require_roles("admin", "finance")` to `get_current_user`.

### 7. UUID Serialization Error in Simulate
Error: `TypeError: Object of type UUID is not JSON serializable` during `payload.model_dump()` for JSONB column.

Fix: Changed `payment.raw_callback = payload.model_dump()` to `payload.model_dump(mode="json")` in `payments/service.py`.

### 8. Booking List Issues
User reported: date shows "undefined", session shows "-", asked what "fee flag" is.

Root causes:
- `BookingResponse` only had `session_id`, no `session_title`/`session_start_time`/`learner_username`
- Repository never loaded `session` or `learner` relationships

**Fixes:**
- `backend/app/modules/bookings/repository.py`: Added `_with_relations()` helper using `selectinload(Booking.session)` and `selectinload(Booking.learner)` applied to `get()` and `list()` queries
- `backend/app/modules/bookings/schemas.py`: Added `session_title`, `session_start_time`, `learner_username` to `BookingResponse` with `@model_validator(mode="before")` to extract from relationships
- `frontend/src/pages/bookings/BookingListPage.vue`: Added `formatInTimeZone`/`parseISO` imports, `fmtDate()` function, renamed "Fee Flag" → "Cancellation Fee", updated `#cell-session_start_time` slot
- Explained "Fee Flag" = `policy_fee_flagged`, set when learner cancels within 24h of session start

### 9. Promotions Showing "No promotions applied"
User reported checkout shows "No promotions applied".

Root cause explained: Course price defaults to 0.0, so discount calculation always returns 0 which is discarded.

Also fixed: `session_title` missing from `OrderItemResponse`:
- `backend/app/modules/checkout/schemas.py`: Added `session_title: str | None = None` to `OrderItemResponse`
- `backend/app/modules/checkout/service.py`: Added batch session title fetch in `get_order()` using `select(Session).where(Session.id.in_(session_ids))`

### 10. Phone and Email Input Missing
User: "there is no input to set learner phone"

Also asked about enrollment status.

**Fixes to `UserFormPage.vue`:**

- On edit load: clear phone/email (masked values) so admin only overwrites if they type new values
- On edit submit: only include email/phone in patch if non-empty
- Added email (type=email) and phone (type=tel) inputs to template with edit hint labels
- `api/endpoints/admin.ts`: No change needed (already `Partial<Course>`)

**Enrollment status explanation:** It's the booking status. Set via Admin confirm action. Displayed in bookings list, session detail roster, session browse capacity bar, advanced search facets.

### 11. Audit Logs Empty
User: "in the audit logs page there is no log even though I have created users, sessions, locations"

Root cause: `log_audit` was never called for user create/update/delete, session create, location create/update/delete.

**Fixes:**
- `backend/app/modules/users/service.py`: Added `log_audit` import, added calls in `create()`, `update()`, `delete()` with `actor_id` parameter
- `backend/app/modules/users/controller.py`: Changed `_=Depends(require_roles("admin"))` to `current_user=Depends(...)` on create/update/delete, passed `str(current_user.id)` as `actor_id`
- `backend/app/modules/sessions/service.py`: Added `log_audit` call in `create()` using `str(created_by)`
- `backend/app/modules/locations/service.py`: Added `log_audit` import, added calls in `create()`, `update()`, `delete()`, `create_room()`, `update_room()`, `delete_room()` with `actor_id` parameter
- `backend/app/modules/locations/controller.py`: Added `get_current_user` import, changed all mutation endpoints to expose `current_user`, passed `actor_id`

### 12. Audit Log Entity Type Dropdown

Fix: Changed text input to select with options: All, user, session, booking, location, room, refund in `AuditLogPage.vue`.

### 13. UUID Empty String Errors
Two errors from empty string UUID fields:
1. `GET /api/audit-logs?actor_id=` — empty string for UUID
2. `POST /api/search` with `site_id=""` and `instructor_id=""`

**Fixes:**
- `frontend/src/api/endpoints/admin.ts` `getAuditLogs`: Strip empty/null values before sending
- `frontend/src/api/endpoints/search.ts` `search`: Strip empty/null values before sending

### 14. Search 500 Error - Auto-correlation
`sqlalchemy.exc.InvalidRequestError: Select statement returned no FROM clauses due to auto-correlation`

Root cause: `select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery())` where `q` contains correlated scalar subqueries. When wrapped in subquery, SQLAlchemy auto-correlates inner queries to outer, removing their FROM clauses.

**Fix:**
- `backend/app/modules/search/service.py`: Added `_build_count_query(filters)` function with same joins/filters but only `func.count(Booking.id)` — no scalar subquery columns

### 15. Phone-only Search 500 Error - Same Auto-correlation
Same error on the data query itself (line 146).

Root cause: `instructor_name` subquery references `User` which is also in outer query → auto-correlated. `order_id` subquery references `Booking` → auto-correlated.

**Fix:**
- Added `.correlate(Instructor)` to instructor_name subquery
- Added `.correlate(Booking)` to order_id subquery
- Removed unnecessary `func.concat()` wrapper around instructor subquery

### 16. Search Missing Fields
User reported: `session_title` shows `—`, `session_date` shows `—`, `status` shows `undefined`, `amount` shows `$NaN`

Root cause: Field name mismatches between backend and frontend:
- Backend `course_title` vs frontend key `session_title`
- Backend `session_start` vs frontend key `session_date`  
- Backend `enrollment_status` vs frontend key `status`
- Backend missing `amount` field entirely

**Fixes:**
- `backend/app/modules/search/schemas.py`: Renamed `enrollment_status`→`status`, `session_start`→`session_date`, `course_title`→`session_title`, added `amount: float | None`
- `backend/app/modules/search/service.py`: Updated SQL labels to match, added amount subquery (Order.total where status=paid, correlate Booking), updated row mapping
- `frontend/src/stores/search.ts`: Added `session_id` and `learner_id` to `SearchResult` interface
- `frontend/src/pages/search/AdvancedSearchPage.vue`: Added `formatInTimeZone`/`parseISO` imports, `fmtDate()`, `#cell-session_date` slot, fixed `fmt()` to handle undefined

### 17. Jobs Tab Empty - Current Discussion
User asked: "the jobs tab is empty based on the prompt what should be happening there?"

Context from prompt.md: "A built-in job scheduler orchestrates recurring tasks (attendance rollups, exports, ingestion, reconciliation) with queue-based retries using exponential backoff, and the admin console provides job throughput, latency, and success rate; when failures exceed 2% over the last hour or a job is late by 10 minutes, the system raises an in-app alert and records aggregated error logs for troubleshooting."

The jobs tab (`JobMonitorPage.vue`) shows job stats from `GET /api/jobs/stats/aggregate` and job executions from `GET /api/jobs/{job_name}/executions`. The page is empty because the `job_executions` table has no data — Celery tasks haven't run yet (no actual executions have been recorded).

The backend is fully implemented:
- 7 Celery tasks: close_expired_orders, attendance_rollup, replay_access_expiry, generate_reconciliation_export, purge_audit_logs, check_job_health, run_ingestion_jobs
- Each task writes to `job_executions` table via `_record_execution()`
- `GET /api/jobs/stats/aggregate` queries `job_executions` and returns throughput/latency/success rate
- Beat schedule drives periodic execution

The tab is empty because no jobs have run yet (celery_beat hasn't triggered them or they haven't completed), so `job_executions` table is empty, and the stats aggregate returns no rows.

The user's question seems to be asking what SHOULD be showing there — the answer is per the prompt: job throughput, latency, success rate per job; in-app alerts when failure rate > 2% or job is late by 10 min.

There might also be a need to add a "manual trigger" button to run jobs on-demand for testing, and possibly show the alerts.

- Fix: Added `@field_validator("applies_to", mode="before")` to `PromotionCreate` and `PromotionUpdate` in `backend/app/modules/promotions/schemas.py` to coerce string `"all"` → `{"all": True}`.

### 2. Port Binding Errors (Docker)
- Port 9090 blocked by Windows → changed Prometheus host port from `9090:9090` to `19090:9090` in `docker-compose.yml`
- Port 9092 blocked by Windows → changed Kafka host port from `9092:9092` to `19092:9092` in `docker-compose.yml`

### 3. How Promotions Work / Manual Testing
User asked how promotions work and how to test via frontend. Explained the automatic best-offer engine, stacking rules, `applies_to` scope, and the full test flow.

### 4. Checkout Flow Implementation (Major Feature)
User reported "only Book Now button that books without payment." After exploring both frontend and backend:

**Backend gaps found:**
- `PaymentService.handle_callback()` raises `NotFoundError` because no `Payment` row is ever created (checkout never creates one)
- No auto-booking creation after payment confirmation
- Three frontend-called endpoints don't exist: `GET /cart/{id}/best-offer`, `POST /cart/{id}/apply-promo`, `POST /cart/{id}/confirm`

**Changes made:**
- `backend/app/modules/checkout/service.py`: Added `Payment` row creation in `create_cart()` after Order is created
- `backend/app/modules/payments/service.py`: Added auto-creation of `Booking` records in `handle_callback()` after payment confirmed
- `frontend/src/api/endpoints/checkout.ts`: Removed 3 dead endpoints; kept `createCart`, `getOrder`, `getOrders`, `cancelOrder`
- `frontend/src/stores/checkout.ts`: Simplified to single `order` ref; `createCart` stores full `OrderResponse` directly
- `frontend/src/pages/learner/CheckoutPage.vue`: Removed promo code section; reads from `checkout.order`; "Proceed to Payment Terminal" button
- `frontend/src/pages/learner/OrderReceiptPage.vue`: Polls `GET /api/orders/{id}` every 3s instead of admin-only payments endpoint
- `frontend/src/pages/sessions/SessionDetailPage.vue`: Fixed `handleBook()` to use checkout flow instead of legacy `bookings.create()`

- Added `price` field to `frontend/src/api/endpoints/admin.ts` Course interface
- Added price input to `CourseListPage.vue` form and price column to table

`payment.raw_callback = payload.model_dump()` — UUID not JSON serializable.

- Added `session_title`, `session_start_time`, `learner_username` to `BookingResponse` with `@model_validator(mode="before")` extracting from loaded relationships

### 9. Promotions Not Showing ("No promotions applied")

- Root cause: Course price defaults to `0.0`; when subtotal is $0, all discounts calculate to $0, which `compute_best_offer` discards

### 14. Search 500 Error (Auto-correlation)
`sqlalchemy.exc.InvalidRequestError: Select statement returned no FROM clauses due to auto-correlation`
- Root cause: `select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery())` — scalar subqueries inside `_build_base_query` auto-correlate when wrapped
- Fix: Added `_build_count_query()` function with same joins/filters but only `func.count(Booking.id)` without scalar subquery columns; used it instead of wrapping the base query

### 15. Search 500 Error (Auto-correlation in data query)
Same error but now on the data query itself at line 146.
- Root cause: `instructor_name` subquery uses `User` which also appears in outer join; `order_id` subquery correlates to `Booking`
- Fix: Added explicit `.correlate(Instructor)` to `instructor_name` subquery and `.correlate(Booking)` to `order_id` subquery; also removed unnecessary `func.concat()` wrapper

### 16. Search Missing Fields
User reported: `session_title` shows `—`, `session_date` shows `—`, `status` shows `undefined`, `amount` shows `$NaN`

- Fixed `backend/app/modules/search/schemas.py`: Renamed fields in `SearchResultItem` to match frontend: `enrollment_status` → `status`, `session_start` → `session_date`, `course_title` → `session_title`; added `amount: float | None`
- Fixed `backend/app/modules/search/service.py`: Updated SQL labels to match new names; added `amount` subquery (Order.total where status=paid); updated row mapping
- Fixed `frontend/src/stores/search.ts`: Added `session_id` and `learner_id` to `SearchResult` interface
- Fixed `frontend/src/pages/search/AdvancedSearchPage.vue`: Added date formatting imports, `fmtDate()` function, `#cell-session_date` template slot; fixed `fmt()` to handle `undefined`

### 17. Jobs Tab Empty
User asked what should be happening in the jobs tab based on the prompt.
- The jobs tab shows job monitor: throughput, latency, success rates from `job_executions` table
- The page is correctly implemented; it's empty because Celery tasks haven't run yet (no `JobExecution` rows)
- From the prompt: "admin console provides job throughput, latency, and success rate; when failures exceed 2% over the last hour or a job is late by 10 minutes, system raises an in-app alert"
- Jobs that exist: `close_expired_orders`, `attendance_rollup`, `replay_access_expiry`, `generate_reconciliation_export`, `purge_audit_logs`, `check_job_health`, `run_ingestion_jobs`
- The tab is empty simply because none of these Celery tasks have run yet (system just started)
- There's also a bug in the controller: `last_status` subquery uses `JobExecution.job_name == JobExecution.job_name` (always true, not correlated) — should be a parameter

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **Promotion `applies_to` dict validation error**: Frontend sends `"all"` (string), backend expects dict. Fixed with Pydantic `field_validator(mode="before")` coercing strings.

- **Docker port 9090/9092 blocked on Windows**: Hyper-V/ICS reserves these ports. Fixed by remapping to `19090:9090` and `19092:9092`.

- **Checkout endpoints 404**: Frontend called `cart/{id}/best-offer`, `cart/{id}/apply-promo`, `cart/{id}/confirm` — none exist. Fixed by removing these calls; `create_cart` already returns full order with discounts.

- **Payment callback `NotFoundError`**: `handle_callback` tried to find existing Payment but none was created. Fixed by creating `Payment(status=pending)` in `create_cart`.

- **UUID JSON serialization error in simulate**: `payload.model_dump()` returns UUID objects. Fixed with `payload.model_dump(mode="json")`.

- **OrderReceiptPage polling 403**: Was polling `GET /api/payments/{order_id}` (admin/finance only). Fixed to poll `GET /api/orders/{id}` (any authenticated user).

- **Terminal simulator 403 for learners**: Initially restricted endpoint to admin/finance. User correctly pointed out learners can't pay then. Fixed to `get_current_user` (any authenticated user).

- **Booking list session/date showing `-`/`undefined`**: Repository never loaded relationships; `BookingResponse` had no denormalized fields. Fixed with `selectinload` in repository and `@model_validator` in schema.

- **Promotions not applying ($0 discount)**: Course price defaults to `0.0`, making all discounts $0 which are discarded. Told user to set non-zero course price; also fixed missing `session_title` in order items.

- **Search 500 auto-correlation (count query)**: `select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery())` fails when base query has correlated scalar subqueries. Fixed with dedicated `_build_count_query()` without scalar subquery columns.

- **Search 500 auto-correlation (data query)**: `instructor_name` subquery uses `User` table which appears in outer join; `order_id` references `Booking`. Fixed with explicit `.correlate(Instructor)` and `.correlate(Booking)`.

- **Search empty string UUID errors**: Frontend sent `site_id=""`, `instructor_id=""`, `actor_id=""`. Fixed by stripping empty/null values before API calls in both `search.ts` and `admin.ts`.

- **Search missing fields**: Backend field names (`course_title`, `session_start`, `enrollment_status`) didn't match frontend column keys (`session_title`, `session_date`, `status`); `amount` field didn't exist. Fixed by renaming in backend schema/service and adding amount subquery.

- **Audit logs empty**: `log_audit` never called for user create/update/delete, session create, or any location operations. Fixed by adding `log_audit` calls and exposing `current_user` in controllers for all missing operations.

5. Problem Solving:

- **Checkout flow gap**: Most complex fix — identified that the frontend had 3 non-existent endpoint calls, backend never created Payment rows, and payment callback never created bookings. Solved by simplifying frontend to use what exists (create_cart returns full order) and wiring the missing backend logic.

- **SQLAlchemy auto-correlation**: Two-part fix — `_build_count_query` for count operations, explicit `.correlate()` on scalar subqueries for data operations. The root cause was SQLAlchemy seeing shared table references between outer and inner queries and stripping FROM clauses.

- **Jobs tab empty**: The jobs tab is correctly implemented. It's empty because Celery beat tasks haven't executed yet (no `job_executions` rows). The tab will populate once: celery_worker processes tasks, celery_beat triggers scheduled tasks. There is also a bug in the `last_status` subquery in `controller.py` — `JobExecution.job_name == JobExecution.job_name` is always true (should be correlated to the outer group's job_name).

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **Promotion `applies_to` dict validation error**: Frontend sends `"all"` (string), backend expects dict. Fixed with Pydantic `field_validator(mode="before")` coercing strings.

- **Docker port 9090/9092 blocked on Windows**: Hyper-V/ICS reserves these ports. Fixed by remapping to `19090:9090` and `19092:9092`.

- **Checkout endpoints 404**: Frontend called `cart/{id}/best-offer`, `cart/{id}/apply-promo`, `cart/{id}/confirm` — none exist. Fixed by removing these calls; `create_cart` already returns full order with discounts.

- **Payment callback `NotFoundError`**: `handle_callback` tried to find existing Payment but none was created. Fixed by creating `Payment(status=pending)` in `create_cart`.

- **UUID JSON serialization error in simulate**: `payload.model_dump()` returns UUID objects. Fixed with `payload.model_dump(mode="json")`.

- **OrderReceiptPage polling 403**: Was polling `GET /api/payments/{order_id}` (admin/finance only). Fixed to poll `GET /api/orders/{id}` (any authenticated user).

- **Terminal simulator 403 for learners**: Initially restricted endpoint to admin/finance. User correctly pointed out learners can't pay then. Fixed to `get_current_user` (any authenticated user).

- **Booking list session/date showing `-`/`undefined`**: Repository never loaded relationships; `BookingResponse` had no denormalized fields. Fixed with `selectinload` in repository and `@model_validator` in schema.

- **Promotions not applying ($0 discount)**: Course price defaults to `0.0`, making all discounts $0 which are discarded. Told user to set non-zero course price; also fixed missing `session_title` in order items.

- **Search 500 auto-correlation (count query)**: `select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery())` fails when base query has correlated scalar subqueries. Fixed with dedicated `_build_count_query()` without scalar subquery columns.

- **Search 500 auto-correlation (data query)**: `instructor_name` subquery uses `User` table which appears in outer join; `order_id` references `Booking`. Fixed with explicit `.correlate(Instructor)` and `.correlate(Booking)`.

- **Search empty string UUID errors**: Frontend sent `site_id=""`, `instructor_id=""`, `actor_id=""`. Fixed by stripping empty/null values before API calls in both `search.ts` and `admin.ts`.

- **Search missing fields**: Backend field names (`course_title`, `session_start`, `enrollment_status`) didn't match frontend column keys (`session_title`, `session_date`, `status`); `amount` field didn't exist. Fixed by renaming in backend schema/service and adding amount subquery.

- **Audit logs empty**: `log_audit` never called for user create/update/delete, session create, or any location operations. Fixed by adding `log_audit` calls and exposing `current_user` in controllers for all missing operations.

5. Problem Solving:

Solved by simplifying frontend to use what exists (create_cart returns full order) and wiring the missing backend logic.

- **SQLAlchemy auto-correlation**: Two-part fix — `_build_count_query` for count operations, explicit `.correlate()` on scalar subqueries for data operations. The root cause was SQLAlchemy seeing shared table references between outer and inner queries and stripping FROM clauses.

- **Jobs tab empty**: The jobs tab is correctly implemented. It's empty because Celery beat tasks haven't executed yet (no `job_executions` rows). The tab will populate once: celery_worker processes tasks, celery_beat triggers scheduled tasks. Now, the tab is still empty because the Celery tasks haven't run yet. To populate it, trigger a task manually:

```bash
docker compose exec celery_worker celery -A app.modules.jobs.celery_app.celery_app call app.modules.jobs.tasks.close_expired_orders
```

Or trigger several at once:
```bash
docker compose exec celery_worker celery -A app.modules.jobs.celery_app.celery_app call app.modules.jobs.tasks.attendance_rollup
docker compose exec celery_worker celery -A app.modules.jobs.celery_app.celery_app call app.modules.jobs.tasks.check_job_health
```

After they run, `job_executions` rows are inserted and the Jobs tab will show throughput, duration, success rate, and last status. The two bugs fixed:

1. **`last_status` subquery** — was `job_name == job_name` (always true, returning arbitrary row). Now uses `aliased(JobExecution)` as the inner table correlated to the outer group's `job_name`.
2. **`p95_duration_ms`** — was hardcoded `0.0`. Now uses PostgreSQL's `percentile_cont(0.95)` ordered-set aggregate.

    26	@router.get("/stats/aggregate", response_model=JobStatsAggregate)
    27	async def get_job_stats(
    28	    window_minutes: int = Query(60, ge=1, le=1440),
    29	    _=Depends(require_roles("admin", "dataops")),
    30	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    31	):
    32	    """
    33	    Admin console endpoint: job throughput, latency, success rate.
    34	    Queries job_executions table directly — not Grafana.
    35	    """
    36	    since = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(minutes=window_minutes)
    37	
    38	    je_inner = aliased(JobExecution)
    39	    result = await db.execute(
    40	        select(
    41	            JobExecution.job_name,
    42	            func.count().label("total"),
    43	            func.sum((JobExecution.status == "success").cast(func.Integer)).label("success_count"),
    44	            func.sum((JobExecution.status == "failure").cast(func.Integer)).label("failure_count"),
    45	            func.avg(
    46	                func.extract(
    47	                    "epoch",
    48	                    JobExecution.finished_at - JobExecution.started_at
    49	                ) * 1000
    50	            ).label("avg_duration_ms"),
    51	            func.percentile_cont(0.95).within_group(
    52	                func.extract("epoch", JobExecution.finished_at - JobExecution.started_at) * 1000
    53	            ).label("p95_duration_ms"),
    54	            func.max(JobExecution.started_at).label("last_run_at"),
    55	            (select(je_inner.status)
    56	             .where(je_inner.job_name == JobExecution.job_name)
    57	             .order_by(je_inner.started_at.desc())
    58	             .limit(1)
    59	             .correlate(JobExecution)
    60	             .scalar_subquery()
    61	            ).label("last_status"),
    62	        )
    63	        .where(JobExecution.started_at >= since)
    64	        .group_by(JobExecution.job_name)
    65	    )
    66	    rows = result.all()
    67	
    68	    stats: list[JobStatRow] = []
    69	    for row in rows:
    70	        total = row.total or 0
    71	        success = row.success_count or 0
    72	        failure = row.failure_count or 0
    73	        stats.append(JobStatRow(
    74	            job_name=row.job_name,
    75	            total_executions=total,
    76	            success_count=success,
    77	            failure_count=failure,
    78	            success_rate_pct=round(success / total * 100, 1) if total else 0.0,
    79	            avg_duration_ms=round(float(row.avg_duration_ms or 0), 1),
    80	            p95_duration_ms=round(float(row.p95_duration_ms or 0), 1),
    81	            last_run_at=row.last_run_at,
    82	            last_status=row.last_status,
    83	        ))
    84	
    85	    return JobStatsAggregate(window_minutes=window_minutes, jobs=stats)

     2	import { ref } from 'vue'
     3	import { jobsApi } from '@/api/endpoints/jobs'
     4	
     5	export interface JobStat {
     6	  job_name: string
     7	  total_executions: number
     8	  success_count: number
     9	  failure_count: number
    10	  success_rate_pct: number
    11	  avg_duration_ms: number
    12	  p95_duration_ms: number
    13	  last_run_at: string | null
    14	  last_status: 'success' | 'failure' | null
    15	}
    16	
    17	export interface JobExecution {
    18	  id: string
    19	  job_name: string
    20	  started_at: string
    21	  finished_at: string | null
    22	  status: 'success' | 'failure' | 'late'
    23	  error_detail: string | null
    24	}
    25	
    26	export interface Alert {
    27	  id: string
    28	  type: string
    29	  message: string
    30	  job_name: string | null
    31	  created_at: string
    32	}
    33	
    34	export const useJobsStore = defineStore('jobs', () => {
    35	  const stats = ref<{ window_minutes: number; jobs: JobStat[] } | null>(null)
    36	  const executions = ref<JobExecution[]>([])
    37	  const alerts = ref<Alert[]>([])
    38	  const health = ref<{ status: string; timestamp: string } | null>(null)
    39	  const loading = ref(false)
    40	
    41	  async function fetchStats(windowMinutes = 60) {
    42	    loading.value = true
    43	    try {
    44	      stats.value = await jobsApi.getStats(windowMinutes)
    45	    } finally {
    46	      loading.value = false
    47	    }
    48	  }
    49	
    50	  async function fetchExecutions(jobName: string) {

    53	
    54	  async function fetchAlerts() {
    55	    alerts.value = await jobsApi.getAlerts()
    56	  }
    57	
    58	  async function fetchHealth() {
    59	    health.value = await jobsApi.getHealth()
    60	  }
    61	
    62	  return { stats, executions, alerts, health, loading, fetchStats, fetchExecutions, fetchAlerts, fetchHealth }
    63	})

    22	    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
    23	        try:

    35	async def _record_execution(job_name: str, started_at: datetime, status: str, error: str | None = None):
    36	    from app.modules.jobs.models import JobExecution
    37	    async with _get_db() as db:
    38	        exec_row = JobExecution(
    39	            job_name=job_name,
    40	            started_at=started_at,
    41	            finished_at=datetime.now(UTC),
    42	            status=status,
    43	            error_detail=error,

    45	        db.add(exec_row)

    53	    name="app.modules.jobs.tasks.close_expired_orders",
    54	    bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=30,
    55	)
    56	def close_expired_orders(self):
    57	    started = datetime.now(UTC)
    58	    try:
    59	        _run_async(_close_expired_orders_async())
    60	        _run_async(_record_execution("close_expired_orders", started, "success"))

**Backend** — `POST /api/jobs/{job_name}/trigger` (admin/dataops only): looks up the task name from the beat schedule (accepts either the friendly key like `close-expired-orders` or the full task name), then calls `celery_app.send_task()` to queue it immediately.

**Frontend** — each row in the Job Monitor table now has a "Run" button. Clicking it:
1. Sends the trigger request
2. Shows a toast (`Job "..." queued`)
3. Refreshes the stats after 2 seconds so the new execution appears

The button is disabled while that specific job is triggering (other rows stay enabled).
The backend `ExportRequest` expects `{ filters: {...}, format: "..." }` but the frontend spreads filters flat. Also `'xlsx'` needs to map to `'excel'`. Let me check the export composable too:

async def _check_job_health_async():
    from sqlalchemy import select, func, and_
    from app.core.config import settings
    from app.modules.jobs.models import JobExecution, MonitoringAlert

    async with _get_db() as db:
        window_start = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(hours=1)

        # Get job stats per name in last hour
        result = await db.execute(
            select(
                JobExecution.job_name,
                func.count().label("total"),
                func.sum((JobExecution.status == "failure").cast(int)).label("failures"),
                func.max(JobExecution.started_at).label("last_run"),
            )
            .where(JobExecution.started_at >= window_start)
            .group_by(JobExecution.job_name)
        )
        stats = result.all()

        for row in stats:
            failure_rate = (row.failures / row.total * 100) if row.total else 0
            if failure_rate > settings.JOB_FAILURE_RATE_THRESHOLD_PCT:
                alert = MonitoringAlert(
                    alert_type="high_failure_rate",
                    message=f"Job '{row.job_name}' failure rate: {failure_rate:.1f}% in last hour.",
                    job_name=row.job_name,
                )
                db.add(alert)
                logger.warning("ALERT: %s failure rate %.1f%%", row.job_name, failure_rate)

        # Check for late jobs (expected every N minutes but haven't run)
        # We infer expected schedule from beat_schedule config
        from app.modules.jobs.celery_app import celery_app as _ca
        for task_name, schedule_conf in _ca.conf.beat_schedule.items():
            sched = schedule_conf.get("schedule")
            if isinstance(sched, (int, float)):
                interval_minutes = sched / 60
                expected_latest = datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(minutes=interval_minutes + settings.JOB_LATENESS_THRESHOLD_MINUTES)
                job_key = schedule_conf.get("task", "").split(".")[-1]
                last_run_result = await db.execute(
                    select(func.max(JobExecution.started_at)).where(JobExecution.job_name == job_key)
                )
                last_run = last_run_result.scalar_one()
                if last_run and last_run < expected_latest:
                    alert = MonitoringAlert(
                        alert_type="job_late",
                        message=f"Job '{job_key}' last ran at {last_run.isoformat()}, expected by {expected_latest.isoformat()}.",
                        job_name=job_key,

     4	export const searchApi = {
     5	  search: async (params: SearchFilters & { include_facets?: boolean }) => {
     6	    const clean = Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(params).filter(([, v]) => v !== '' && v != null))
     7	    const res = await client.post('/api/search', clean)
     8	    return res.data as { results: SearchResult[]; total_count: number; query_time_ms: number; facets?: SearchFacets }
     9	  },
    10	  getSaved: async () => {
    11	    const res = await client.get('/api/search/saved')
    12	    return res.data as SavedSearch[]
    13	  },
    14	  saveSearch: async (name: string, filters: SearchFilters) => {
    15	    const res = await client.post('/api/search/saved', { name, filters_json: filters })
    16	    return res.data as SavedSearch
    17	  },
    18	  deleteSearch: async (id: string) => {
    19	    await client.delete(`/api/search/saved/${id}`)
    20	  },
    21	  export: async (filters: SearchFilters, format: 'csv' | 'xlsx') => {
    22	    const res = await client.post('/api/search/export', { ...filters, format }, { responseType: 'blob' })
    23	    return res
    24	  },
    25	}

34	def filter_duplicates(rows: list[dict], source_id: str, redis_client) -> list[dict]:
35	    """Remove already-seen rows using Redis dedup set."""
36	    fresh = []
37	    pipe = redis_client.pipeline()
38	    for row in rows:
39	        fp = _row_fingerprint(row)
40	        key = f"ingest_dedup:{source_id}:{fp}"
41	        pipe.set(key, "1", ex=604800, nx=True)  # 7-day TTL, only set if not exists
42	    results = pipe.execute()
43	    for row, was_new in zip(rows, results):
44	        if was_new:
45	            fresh.append(row)
46	    return fresh

23	    def __init__(self) -> None:
24	        # session_id → {user_id: websocket}

26	
27	    async def connect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
28	        await ws.accept()
29	        self._rooms[session_id][user_id] = ws
30	        logger.debug("WS connected: session=%s user=%s total=%d", session_id, user_id, len(self._rooms[session_id]))
31	
32	    def disconnect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str) -> None:
33	        self._rooms[session_id].pop(user_id, None)
34	        if not self._rooms[session_id]:
35	            self._rooms.pop(session_id, None)
36

content.text:
Now I have enough to give a precise verdict and plan.

---

## Verification: All 14 Issues Confirmed

Every reported issue exists. Here's a fix plan grouped by effort:

---

## Fix Plan

### Blocker Group 1 — Finance contract mismatches (4 fixes)

**B1. Refund `order_id` → `payment_id`**
Easiest fix is on the backend: in `PaymentService.create_refund`, look up the payment by `order_id` if `payment_id` is absent. Change `RefundCreate` to accept `order_id: uuid.UUID` as an alternative, resolve to `payment_id` in the service. Frontend keeps sending `order_id`.

**B2. Missing `GET /api/refunds` list**
Add to `payments/controller.py`:
```python
@router.get("/refunds", response_model=list[RefundResponse])
async def list_refunds(status: str | None = None, _=Depends(require_roles("admin","finance")), db=...):
    return await PaymentService(db).list_refunds(status)
```
Add `list_refunds` to `PaymentService`.

**B3. Reconciliation export: GET vs POST**
Frontend uses `GET /api/reconciliation/export`. Add a `GET` alias in the controller (keeps existing POST too).

**B4. Missing `/reconciliation/exports/{id}/download` route**
Add:
```python
@router.get("/reconciliation/exports/{export_id}/download")
async def download_export(export_id: uuid.UUID, ...):
```
Stream the file from MinIO using a presigned URL redirect.

---

### Blocker Group 2 — Search contract mismatches (3 fixes)

**B5. Saved search `filters_json` vs `filters`**
Fix frontend `search.ts:15`: change `{ name, filters_json: filters }` → `{ name, filters }`.

**B6. Search export in `searchApi.export` still flattened**
`useExport.ts` is fixed already. Fix `searchApi.export` in `search.ts` to also wrap correctly (dead code path, but clean it up):
```ts
export: async (filters, format) => {
  const clean = Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(filters).filter(([,v]) => v !== '' && v != null))
  return client.post('/api/search/export', { filters: clean, format: format === 'xlsx' ? 'excel' : format }, { responseType: 'blob' })
}
```

**B7. Date filter `date_from`/`date_to` vs nested `date_range`**
Backend fix (simpler): add `date_from` / `date_to` flat fields to `SearchFilters` schema and handle them in the service alongside `date_range`:
```python
date_from: datetime | None = None
date_to: datetime | None = None
```
Service: `if filters.date_from: q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_from)` etc.

---

### Blocker Group 3 — IDOR (2 fixes)

**B8. Booking ownership**
In `BookingService.get`, `reschedule`, `cancel`: after fetching booking, if caller role is `learner`, assert `booking.learner_id == UUID(actor_id)` else raise `ForbiddenError`.
Controller must pass `current_user` to these service calls (already has `actor_id` for reschedule/cancel, just needs the role too).

**B9. Order ownership**
In `CheckoutService.get_order`, `cancel_order`: accept optional `caller_id` + `caller_role`; if role is `learner`, assert `order.learner_id == caller_id`.

---

### High — Ingestion (3 fixes)

**H1. Webhook API key auth**
Add header dependency to the webhook endpoint:
```python
from fastapi import Header
async def webhook_ingest(source_id, payload, x_api_key: str = Header(...), db=...):
    source = await IngestionService(db).get_source(source_id)
    config = json.loads(decrypt_field(source.config_encrypted))
    if config.get("api_key") != x_api_key:
        raise ForbiddenError()
```

**H2. logstash + batch_file support + dedup wiring**
In `run_source`:
- `logstash` branch: records are already delivered via webhook `handle_webhook`; `run_source` for logstash should be a no-op or pull from a queue.
- `batch_file` branch: fetch the file from MinIO, parse via `batch_adapter.parse_file`, run `filter_duplicates` via Redis, ingest rows.
- Add `run.status = IngestionRunStatus.success` before the except block (currently missing).

**H3. Promotion audit logging**
In `promotions/controller.py`, expose `current_user` on create/update/delete and call `log_audit`. Same pattern already done for users/locations.

---

### High — Missing endpoints (1 fix)

**H4. Booking history endpoint**
Add `GET /api/bookings/{id}/history` to the bookings controller. Source data: query `audit_logs` where `entity_type="booking"` and `entity_id=str(booking_id)`, ordered by `created_at desc`. Return as a list.

---

### Medium (4 fixes)

**M1. Recurring session title**
`sessions/service.py:70` — add `title=payload.title` to the `Session(...)` constructor.

**M2. Schedule overlap check**
In `SessionService.create` and `create_recurring`, after resolving room, query for any session in the same room whose `[start_time, end_time + buffer_minutes]` overlaps the new slot. Raise `ConflictError` if found.

**M3. WebSocket blocklist check**
In `deps.py::get_ws_user`, add after token decode:
```python
if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
    await websocket.close(code=4001)
    raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
```

**M4. Test login form → JSON**

## M2 — Schedule overlap/buffer conflict check

**File:** `backend/app/modules/sessions/service.py`
Read the `create` and `create_recurring` methods.

Add a helper method `_check_room_conflict` to `SessionService`:
```python
async def _check_room_conflict(self, room_id: uuid.UUID, start: datetime, end: datetime, buffer: int, exclude_session_id=None):
    from sqlalchemy import select, and_, or_
    from app.modules.sessions.models import Session as SessionModel, SessionStatus
    from datetime import timedelta
    buffered_end = end + timedelta(minutes=buffer)
    q = (
        select(SessionModel)
        .where(
            SessionModel.room_id == room_id,
            SessionModel.status.not_in([SessionStatus.cancelled]),
            or_(
                and_(SessionModel.start_time < buffered_end, SessionModel.end_time > start),
            )
        )
    )
    if exclude_session_id:
        q = q.where(SessionModel.id != exclude_session_id)
    result = await self._db.execute(q)
    conflict = result.scalar_one_or_none()
    if conflict:
        from app.core.exceptions import ConflictError
        raise ConflictError(f"Room is already booked from {conflict.start_time} to {conflict.end_time} (buffer included).")
```

Call `await self._check_room_conflict(payload.room_id, payload.start_time, payload.end_time, payload.buffer_minutes)` in `create()` before creating the session.

Call it in `create_recurring()` for each date before creating each session.

Also call it in `update()` if `start_time`, `end_time`, or `room_id` is being changed.

## M2 — Schedule overlap/buffer conflict check

**File:** `backend/app/modules/sessions/service.py`
Read the `create` and `create_recurring` methods.

Add a helper method `_check_room_conflict` to `SessionService`:
```python
async def _check_room_conflict(self, room_id: uuid.UUID, start: datetime, end: datetime, buffer: int, exclude_session_id=None):
    from sqlalchemy import select, and_, or_
    from app.modules.sessions.models import Session as SessionModel, SessionStatus
    from datetime import timedelta
    buffered_end = end + timedelta(minutes=buffer)
    q = (
        select(SessionModel)
        .where(
            SessionModel.room_id == room_id,
            SessionModel.status.not_in([SessionStatus.cancelled]),
            or_(
                and_(SessionModel.start_time < buffered_end, SessionModel.end_time > start),
            )
        )
    )
    if exclude_session_id:
        q = q.where(SessionModel.id != exclude_session_id)
    result = await self._db.execute(q)
    conflict = result.scalar_one_or_none()
    if conflict:
        from app.core.exceptions import ConflictError
        raise ConflictError(f"Room is already booked from {conflict.start_time} to {conflict.end_time} (buffer included).")
```

Call `await self._check_room_conflict(payload.room_id, payload.start_time, payload.end_time, payload.buffer_minutes)` in `create()` before creating the session.

## M2 — Schedule overlap/buffer conflict check

**File:** `backend/app/modules/sessions/service.py`
Add helper method to `SessionService`:
```python
async def _check_room_conflict(
    self,
    room_id: uuid.UUID,
    start: datetime,
    end: datetime,
    buffer_minutes: int,
    exclude_id=None,
):
    from datetime import timedelta
    buffered_end = end + timedelta(minutes=buffer_minutes)
    from sqlalchemy import and_, or_
    from app.modules.sessions.models import SessionStatus
    q = (
        select(Session)
        .where(Session.room_id == room_id)
        .where(Session.status != SessionStatus.cancelled)
        .where(
            and_(Session.start_time < buffered_end, Session.end_time > start)
        )
    )
    if exclude_id:
        q = q.where(Session.id != exclude_id)
    result = await self._db.execute(q)
    conflict = result.scalar_one_or_none()
    if conflict:
        raise ConflictError(
            f"Room conflict: session already scheduled {conflict.start_time}–{conflict.end_time} (including buffer)."
        )
```

Call `await self._check_room_conflict(...)` in:
- `create()` before creating the Session
- `create_recurring()` for each date slot before creating the Session
- `update()` if `room_id`, `start_time`, or `end_time` is being changed (pass `exclude_id=session_id`)

## M2 — Schedule overlap/buffer conflict check

**File:** `backend/app/modules/sessions/service.py`
Add helper method to `SessionService`:
```python
async def _check_room_conflict(
    self,
    room_id: uuid.UUID,
    start: datetime,
    end: datetime,
    buffer_minutes: int,
    exclude_id=None,
):
    from datetime import timedelta
    buffered_end = end + timedelta(minutes=buffer_minutes)
    from sqlalchemy import and_, or_
    from app.modules.sessions.models import SessionStatus
    q = (
        select(Session)
        .where(Session.room_id == room_id)
        .where(Session.status != SessionStatus.cancelled)
        .where(
            and_(Session.start_time < buffered_end, Session.end_time > start)
        )
    )
    if exclude_id:
        q = q.where(Session.id != exclude_id)
    result = await self._db.execute(q)
    conflict = result.scalar_one_or_none()
    if conflict:
        raise ConflictError(
            f"Room conflict: session already scheduled {conflict.start_time}–{conflict.end_time} (including buffer)."
        )
```

Call `await self._check_room_conflict(...)` in:
- `create()` before creating the Session

1	"""MinIO object storage client helpers."""
     2	
     3	from datetime import timedelta
     4	from io import BytesIO
     5	
     6	from minio import Minio
     7	from minio.error import S3Error
     8	
     9	from app.core.config import settings
    10	
    11	_client: Minio | None = None
    12	
    13	
    14	def get_minio_client() -> Minio:
    15	    global _client
    16	    if _client is None:
    17	        _client = Minio(
    18	            settings.MINIO_ENDPOINT,
    19	            access_key=settings.MINIO_ACCESS_KEY,
    20	            secret_key=settings.MINIO_SECRET_KEY,
    21	            secure=settings.MINIO_USE_SSL,
    22	        )
    23	    return _client
    24	
    25	
    26	def ensure_bucket_exists(bucket: str) -> None:
    27	    client = get_minio_client()
    28	    if not client.bucket_exists(bucket):
    29	        client.make_bucket(bucket)
    30	
    31	
    32	def upload_object(bucket: str, object_key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "video/mp4") -> None:
    33	    client = get_minio_client()
    34	    ensure_bucket_exists(bucket)
    35	    client.put_object(bucket, object_key, BytesIO(data), length=len(data), content_type=content_type)
    36	
    37	
    38	def get_presigned_download_url(bucket: str, object_key: str) -> str:
    39	    client = get_minio_client()
    40	    url = client.presigned_get_object(
    41	        bucket,
    42	        object_key,
    43	        expires=timedelta(seconds=settings.PRESIGNED_URL_EXPIRE_SECONDS),
    44	    )
    45	    # Rewrite the internal MinIO hostname to the public-facing endpoint so
    46	    # browsers can reach the URL (minio:9000 is only resolvable inside Docker).
    47	    public = settings.MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT or settings.MINIO_ENDPOINT
    48	    if public != settings.MINIO_ENDPOINT:
    49	        scheme = "https" if settings.MINIO_USE_SSL else "http"
    50	        url = url.replace(f"{scheme}://{settings.MINIO_ENDPOINT}", f"{scheme}://{public}", 1)
    51	    return url
    52	
    53	
    54	def get_presigned_upload_url(bucket: str, object_key: str) -> str:
    55	    client = get_minio_client()
    56	    ensure_bucket_exists(bucket)
    57	    return client.presigned_put_object(
    58	        bucket,
    59	        object_key,
    60	        expires=timedelta(seconds=settings.PRESIGNED_URL_EXPIRE_SECONDS),
    61	    )
    62	
    63	
    64	def stat_object(bucket: str, object_key: str):
    65	    """Return object stat (has .size and .content_type)."""
    66	    client = get_minio_client()
    67	    return client.stat_object(bucket, object_key)
    68	
    69	
    70	def get_object_chunks(bucket: str, object_key: str, offset: int = 0, length: int | None = None):
    71	    """Generator that yields raw bytes from MinIO, supporting byte-range reads."""
    72	    client = get_minio_client()
    73	    kwargs: dict = {}
    74	    if offset:
    75	        kwargs["offset"] = offset
    76	    if length is not None:
    77	        kwargs["length"] = length
    78	    response = client.get_object(bucket, object_key, **kwargs)
    79	    try:
    80	        for chunk in response.stream(amt=64 * 1024):
    81	            yield chunk
    82	    finally:
    83	        response.close()
    84	        response.release_conn()
    85	
    86	
    87	def delete_object(bucket: str, object_key: str) -> None:
    88	    client = get_minio_client()
    89	    try:
    90	        client.remove_object(bucket, object_key)
    91	    except S3Error:
    92	        pass

    11	
    12	async def log_audit(
    13	    db: AsyncSession,
    14	    actor_id: str,
    15	    entity_type: str,
    16	    entity_id: str,
    17	    action: str,
    18	    old_value: dict[str, Any] | None = None,

    20	    ip_address: str | None = None,
    21	) -> None:
    22	    """Insert an audit_log row. Intentionally fire-and-forget (no raise)."""
    23	    from app.modules.audit.models import AuditLog  # late import to avoid circular

    27	            id=uuid.uuid4(),

    29	            entity_type=entity_type,
    30	            entity_id=entity_id,
    31	            action=action,
    32	            old_value=old_value,
    33	            new_value=new_value,
    34	            ip_address=ip_address,

    32

    34	        if existing:
    35	            raise ConflictError("You already have an active booking for this session.")
    36	
    37	        booking = Booking(session_id=payload.session_id, learner_id=learner_id)

    43	        if not booking:
    44	            raise NotFoundError("Booking")
    45	        if booking.status != BookingStatus.pending:
    46	            raise ConflictError(f"Cannot confirm booking with status {booking.status}.")
    47	
    48	        booking.status = BookingStatus.confirmed
    49	        booking.confirmed_at = datetime.now(UTC)
    50	        await self._repo.save(booking)

    53	        session = await self._session_repo.get(booking.session_id)
    54	        if session:
    55	            session.enrolled_count += 1

    58	                "type": "roster_update",
    59	                "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count,
    60	            })
    61	
    62	        await log_audit(self._db, actor_id, "booking", str(booking_id), "confirm")
    63	        return BookingResponse.model_validate(booking)
    64	
    65	    async def reschedule(self, booking_id: uuid.UUID, payload: RescheduleRequest, actor_id: str) -> BookingResponse:
    66	        booking = await self._repo.get(booking_id)
    67	        if not booking:
    68	            raise NotFoundError("Booking")
    69	
    70	        if booking.status not in (BookingStatus.pending, BookingStatus.confirmed):
    71	            raise ConflictError(f"Cannot reschedule booking with status {booking.status}.")
    72	
    73	        # 2-hour block rule

    76	            raise UnprocessableError("Reschedule is blocked within 2 hours of session start.")
    77

    83	
    84	        # Decrement old session count if confirmed

    91	        booking.session_id = payload.new_session_id

    97	        await self._session_repo.save(new_session)
    98	
    99	        await log_audit(self._db, actor_id, "booking", str(booking_id), "reschedule",
   100	                        old_value={"session_id": str(old_session_id)},
   101	                        new_value={"session_id": str(payload.new_session_id)})
   102	        return BookingResponse.model_validate(booking)
   103	
   104	    async def cancel(self, booking_id: uuid.UUID, payload: CancelRequest, actor_id: str, learner_initiated: bool = True) -> BookingResponse:
   105	        booking = await self._repo.get(booking_id)
   106	        if not booking:
   107	            raise NotFoundError("Booking")
   108	
   109	        if booking.status in (BookingStatus.cancelled, BookingStatus.no_show):
   110	            raise ConflictError(f"Booking already in terminal state: {booking.status}.")
   111	
   112	        # 24-hour policy fee check (learner-initiated only)
   113	        session = await self._session_repo.get(booking.session_id)

   115	            if (session.start_time - datetime.now(UTC)) < timedelta(hours=24):
   116	                booking.policy_fee_flagged = True
   117	
   118	        if booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed and session:

   123	                "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count,
   124	            })
   125	
   126	        booking.status = BookingStatus.cancelled
   127	        booking.cancelled_at = datetime.now(UTC)
   128	        booking.cancellation_reason = payload.reason if payload else None
   129	        await self._repo.save(booking)
   130	        await log_audit(self._db, actor_id, "booking", str(booking_id), "cancel")
   131	        return BookingResponse.model_validate(booking)
   132	
   133	    async def list(
   134	        self, learner_id: uuid.UUID | None, session_id: uuid.UUID | None,
   135	        status: BookingStatus | None, page: int, page_size: int
